query: SR-03: I cannot remember, but one of our challenges we faced before was that SubagentStart pulls the original agent spawn prompt, commonly "Do the thing", because our protocols have the process defined.  So that actual prompt that we WERE pulling at one time was not useful.  Now, I forget whether we found a solution to this lousy source signal, and what we ended up with most recently.  This end result should be compared against the goal statement for the best signal to use...  Variances: 1. There should be a bound that cascades to to the tool level... There is no need to 2 guards for the same value.  It should be consistent.  If there is an architectural reason why it should be unbounded anywhere, we should discuss it.  2. No reason to store a blank string, IMO.  ONLY reason I can think of would be to make it slightly easier to consume, not having to account for null, but that seems minor.  3. I'm having a discussion with myself on this one.. We came up with a slightly unnatural attepmpt to undersatnd what the agent might be working on... if we could deliver the briefing based on the goal, without having to have the agents perform a context_briefing request, that would be an interesting solution.  4. An addition:  for injection, and context_briefing response, we should include a static but brief Header instruction:  "Use Unimatrix context_get with ID# for more information when relevant".  This prompt should likely be a CONSTANT, but upon injection, the agent immediately knows how to use the list its getting.
